Narrative_Meaning: Jacks signify youth, high spirits, parties, quarrels, protection, privilege and disagreements.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Teacher card represents not just teachers, but schools in general, and the Teachings that the Teacher gives. The Teacher represents patience and courtesy, but is also a taskmaster, that demands you demonstrate what you have learnt.

Significator.Plot: The Teacher Plot seeks to educate and elucidate, sometimes teaching only one side, other times teaching all sides. The lesson may be through the use of metaphor or allegory, more often it is a little more direct. These Plots are based around facts and truisms, often of a religious or moral nature, occasionally of a scientific or spiritual one. The Teacher Plot is also about testing if the student is ready, and rites of passage in general will feature strongly in these tales.
Significator.Side: When one side is marked by the Teacher they are usually tasked with teaching a lesson to another side, often this may be a lesson about humility, but almost any lesson will do.
Significator.Character:
The Teacher Significator indicates a Character who is a teacher.

They Gain Yin when they ask a question or confirm an answer.

Gain Yang when they explain, or answer a question.

They Gain Chi when they set tasks, or someone else asks a question.

They also gain +1 Success Level on any action to do with what they teach.
Significator.Diegetic_Character: A dedicated mentor and educator, possessing a wealth of knowledge and a passion for sharing it with others. They are patient and articulate, always seeking to enlighten and guide those willing to learn, while expecting diligence and respect in return.
Significator.Scene: The Teacher Scene is nothing but a learning moment. A Yarn-Teller can use the card to teach another, or to learn themselves, information will be shared and retained.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Geometry through the Perfect Harmonic. This is a "Perfect" Character for this one, perhaps a lover, friend, mentor or guide, offered up by the plot as part of the Hook.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Story opens with exposition, this may be "incluing" where hints about the world are scattered about the text, or more normally with this card, direct narration that describes the setting. This is usually exposition done by a figure of authority, such as a teacher or politician, reciting the information for the PCs and audience.
Yarn.Gain: Mentor Ally, Learning Descendant, Annex Training or Proficiencies
Yarn.Diegetic_Gain: The guidance of a wise mentor or access to a valuable source of instruction, providing the opportunity to master new skills and gain deeper understanding.
Yarn.Fray_Description: The Study Ordeal consists of studying, researching, experimentation, and perhaps learning. It might be a Teaching Moment in a classroom, a meticulously planned and executed heist, a science experiment or sitting the bar exam. It may even lead to a scientific discovery, or similar paradigm changing moment.
Yarn.Snag_Description: The Embodiment's plans, Descendants, or allies are damaged, or interrupted by disruptive forces. Like the child that behaves inappropriately the moment the teacher's back is turned, there is a disruptive force present that complicates all of the PCs plans, not necessarily out of opposition, but as often boredom, or ignorance.

NPC.Description: The Professor is an educator, always teaching and explaining. Of course, they don't always agree with the latest theories, but anything thirty years old or more is usually accepted.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The Pressed side Gains an Ally who will act as a Trainer, Coach, or Mentor. This leads to additional Trained and Learning Descendants
Yarn.History_Description: The Teacher indicates that the past of this situation goes all the way back to when the characters were being educated. This can mean a call back to something learnt in school, or that this situation has roots in the characters school-days.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Teacher indicates the current situation is similar to a formal examination, this doesn't necessarily mean a written exam, but the character is being tested somehow. Such tests can be about trust, honourableness, worthiness, character or ability as well as academic knowledge.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Teacher indicates that the solution to this problem is not learning some new skill or knowledge, but sharing the knowledge and skills you have with others, this will enable them to learn to feed, defend and improve themselves in the future.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about a Teacher or Mentor that can educate the Characters in some Skill or ability that is often unique to the Setting or Story.
Revelations.Info_Description: Information about a Character that tells you what they are skilled in as well as revealing some of their social circle (school friends) and probably any famous mentors or masters they may have previously had. In some societies knowing what school someone attended, or which wizard taught them their skills can be invaluable in assessing their threat.
Revelations.Vector_Description: The information is announced or pronounced by official sources. Announced by a teacher in form-room or assembly, a priest in a sermon, cried out by a town-crier, or perhaps all over the local press and TV stations, the information is broadcast somewhere the PCs can hear or see it.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is about a method, list, or step-by-step plan. Villains often have a method that they are all too willing to share with their captives as a way of gloating before they finish them off...
Revelations.Detail_Description: A positive detail is something pleasant about an otherwise terrible Character (such as how patient they are, or how charming), Location (Castle Donotenta has the most amazing tapestries on the continent), or Descendant (Well, it's called a Death Ray, but actually it started out as a medical scanner).

Obstacle.Description: The way is blocked by a method that in order to pass it a lesson must first be demonstrated to have been learned. This is usually a simple lesson, like sometimes we have to fail to find a better way.

Tarot.Normal: Target Doubles Chi gained from activated Hitches while atop pile.
Tarot.Crossed: Target may ignores any Triggered Hitch while atop pile, .

Ordeal.Obstacles: 1 Obstacle in each Stage with 2 in all Court Stages
Ordeal.Stage_Description: Leap forwards, across, or skip ahead a Stage or Obstacle if successful.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Get to through the water as fast as you can, skipping anything you can skip. Swim or wade as required.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Flowing side seem close to winning, so they raise the Stakes with a bold assault (Stakes raised to Extreme or Soul-Stakes).
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
A single Monster (usually with at least Two ♦ Monster Facets), that is equivalent Boon Value to any 2 PCs Personality Annex Values added`.
  Facet    Monster  
Awe ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

Tarot Meaning: Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.

Parry:Seize the initiativeThe cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Terror — The Monster is a Fear or Anxiety embodied. They are quite diverse in form, but are always able to cause Psych attacks and Negative Psych effects to any that see them act. They may add Awe Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cause negative emotions in another Character. They automatically draw and play Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) cards as a Psych Attack on any Character that sees them make any attack. They must take Awe Draw cards from their Monster Pool to defend, Soak or Heal any Attack made upon them. Examples of this Monster include Ghosts, some serial killers and some Preternatural Cosmic Horrors.
Craft ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Maker — The Monster is actually a creator of other Monsters. The spawn are often less powerful than the creator, but in some cases are unique and more powerful. The Creator may not be directly encountered until late in the Story. The Creator can draw Craft Draw cards and add them to its Monster Pool each time it creates or summons an Extra or Descendant. At least 1 card must be used from the Monster Pool each Summoning or Creation to help pay the Summoning costs (or as an Obstacle / NPC Yarn card). All currently summoned Extras and Descendants receive a bonus to their Annex Scores and card Pips equal to the Creator’s Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced).
Orthodox ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Parry:Seize the initiativeThe cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Tyrant — The Orthodox Monster is a tyrant, often a part of “the system” (in that it acts as a method of control over a population). A Tyrant monster add Orthodox Draw cards to their Monster Pool every time they are cruel, ruthless, authoritarian or demanding. Tyrants can add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels on another Character’s actions if they order them to do something. Tyrants can also add Monster Pool Size Score Orthodox Failure Levels to an enemy action for a cost of one card from their Monster Pool. The Tyrant must discard half of their their Monster Pool if they are ignored, forgotten or scorned.
Quiet ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Hush — The Quiet Monster is exactly that, almost (or entirely) silent. It is the creeping death of a hidden illness, or the cat-footed predator striking without warning that vanishes without trace. Hush Monsters get to begin with Quiet Score cards in their Monster Pool. Hushes may add +1 Score / Pip for each card in their Monster Pool when they try to act silently. When a Hush creature makes a noise (often a scream or howl, including a Psych attack), it must play at least half of the Monster Pool on the action. Hush Monsters may add cards to their Monster Pool based on how long it has been since the last time they made a noise as a Yarn Extend Duration (e.g. After being silent for a few seconds add a card, after a scene of silence add two. Be silent for a week and add 6 cards to the Pool). Creatures like giant owls, snakes or cats, that stalk and hunt silently are good examples, as is a really creepy, floating mime. Many ghosts are also silent, until they turn and scream to create a Duck attack.
Virtue ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Friendly — The Monster is a “Friendly” monster, that will almost certainly be misunderstood and attacked by some people. They may add Virtue Draw cards to their Monster Pool by being kind to another, or whenever they are attacked. A friendly Monster protects their friends from harm, and must play cards from their Monster Pool to defend, protect, or heal them. Friendly monsters may add a number of Failure Levels equal to the Monster Pool Size Reduced to any enemy that tries to target them or their friends. Friendly Monsters can add Virtue Score (rolled Die or otherwise) to any positive action when playing a card from their Monster Pool. Examples might include a cynical, but big-hearted ogre; a being created from stitched together corpses; a dark-elf ranger with a unicorn necklace; or perhaps even an Electric Mouse.
Zeal ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Celestial — The Monster is a Celestial, a creature of divine or supernatural nature, often associated with a particular divinity, occasionally a political association, they often act as heralds of greater powers. Celestial beings include Anthropomorphic Personifications of social constructs like, Death, War, and Wyrd herself. When acting according to their nature, following their belief system, performing their duties, or following orders, they may draw Zeal Draw Ordeal cards into their Monster Pool. They automatically add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any action to do with their nature, duty, travel, or communication. They also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to actions specific to their nature and duty. Celestial Monsters can only manifest a physical incarnation by playing cards directly from their Monster Pool on all physical actions. The monster could be an angel, a devil, a djinn, or perhaps even one of the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Example: Human / Groonians / Eelafin / Eusaivean / Mer-person / Bigfoot / Giant / Goblin, etc
Lea.Notes: Broccen may use Jack’s to walk human paths (social and technological beings), or to dig new tunnels in the Lea, creating species to fill niches. Jacks may also be used to create Chimerical changes, blending parts of different animals together, each Jack adds another place the Bulmäs can play Lea cards.

Stress.Description: Stress can have profound effects on the mind, causing one to see and hear all sorts of things that are not happening. Memories, waking dreams, and stress can easily combine to create Phantom Characters, hallucinations who take on the appearance of people from the Character's past.
Stress.Rules: The Character’s current Stress and the current Tension Level are added together to create an amount of Chi. This Chi must be paid by the Character and is used to either purchase a "Phantom" version of a Character or create an entirely new "Phantom". The Phantom may haunt the Character, accusing them, often seemingly feeding on the Character's own guilt and continues for a period of time equal to the Current Stress as Intrepid Facet Sway after which the phantom will vanish relieving Stress equal to the current Tension Level. Typically the Phantom will reappear anytime the Character’s current Stress plus the Tension Level is high enough to create them again. Phantoms are typically an extension of the Character, even if they do appear as ghosts, grey aliens, faeries, or shadow people, so trying to harm them with magic, for example, will usually harm the Character experiencing the Phantoms, of course sometimes Increated will use Phantoms as the basis of a Nightmare Form, in which case they can be targeted separately.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can be a teacher, at least there are often lessons to be learned from the traumatic experience. Trauma can teach a Character a new skill, reveal a hidden talent, or unlock a power. Depending upon the genre, trauma may unlock psychic abilities, unusual perceptions or senses, language, art, memory, musical or mathematical abilities.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma allows a Character to purchase with relieved Stress, a new Annex (or just a Proficiency depending on free Slots).

Age.Description: An Age of a Teacher is occurring. During this age a teacher will be revealed within the society, this teacher may act as a leader, guiding the society towards some improvement or advancement, or more often they will train the next generation, inspiring rather than leading, sometimes to be a revolutionary force within the society. It should be noted though, that while the teacher has a profound impact, this is not necessarily and age of learning, and may be a rejection or reaction to the actual teachings.
Age.Diegetic_Description: A time of great instruction and intellectual growth, sparked by the emergence of a visionary teacher. This figure may lead society toward a new dawn or inspire the next generation to challenge old ways, leaving an indelible mark on the culture's development.
Age.Rules: A Character within the society will become a Mentor to either the whole society or to the youth of the society, that will encourage, lead, or inspire improvements, advancements and change in the society.
